Thoroughly scan the original Arleif transcript export for high-judgment user windows. Source root: D:\Downloads\20260618-114047\20260618-114047\.cursor\projects\workspaces-agno\agent-transcripts. Only scan transcript JSONL files whose parent transcript folder UUID begins with 6, 7, 8, 9, a, or b. Include subagents under those parent folders only if the subagent contains user role messages; otherwise ignore subagent-only implementation logs. Use the hybrid v2.5 rubric: identify key_user_move, generic_llm_counterfactual, attribution_basis, likely label, score, portfolio_ready, and why. Return a concise list of all plausible strong_highlight or portfolio candidates, plus the count of user turns scanned and any known existing candidate IDs encountered. Do not modify files.
</user_query>

content.text:
# Arleif Transcript Scan — UUID Prefix `6/7/8/9/a/b`

**Scope:** 42 parent transcripts + 3 subagent files (user messages only) under qualifying folders.  
**User turns scanned:** **225** (222 parent + 3 subagent).  
**Subagents included:** only files with `"role":"user"`; subagent turns were routine CI/review delegation (`below_bar`).

**Known existing candidate IDs encountered (in scope):**
| ID | Window in `Arleif's.md` |
|---|---|
| `a5481311-6fe8-4be2-b534-f38c89ee28c3` | Tool Execution / UI Race Pushback |
| `82a3118c-7689-4255-b2ad-2c638a8e88a0` | Deterministic Board Workflow |

**Known existing IDs filtered out by prefix rule (not scanned):**
- `07653483-…` — Undo Fanout / End-to-End Design  
- `e11ea567-…` — Verify History Before Designing  
- `da4ffd93-…` — Lint Suppression Guardrail  

---

## Portfolio-Ready (in scope)

### 1. Tool Execution / UI Race Pushback — `a5481311`
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Window** | turns ~16–19 (peak: turn 18) |
| **Label / score** | `mindblowing_portfolio` · **10/10** |
| **portfolio_ready** | yes |
| **key_user_move** | Rejected subagent UI tool execution: real-time UI changes before Concierge responds will break provisioner state; skeptical of tools called by an entity other than the one responding with text. |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Would likely accept router/orchestrator coordination and add guardrails; unlikely to derive the text-owner-controls-UI-tools invariant from live SSE timing + prompt imperfection. |
| **attribution_basis** | Assistant had normalized coordinated subagent UI tools; user supplied the race, provisioner break scenario, and ownership standard. |
| **why** | Durable architecture invariant (explicit stateful handoffs); clear artifact path in concierge/streaming design. |

---

## Strong Highlights (in scope, not portfolio-ready)

### 2. Deterministic Board Workflow — `82a3118c`
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Window** | turns 1, 17, 47 |
| **Label / score** | `strong_highlight` · **8/10** |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **key_user_move** | Replace unreliable Project Team leader dispatch with label-gated deterministic workflows; human `rebaseai-design-approved`; require exact tools, sync/async, and who sets completed labels. |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Would optimize leader prompt or add review steps; less likely to remove LLM from the trigger path entirely. |
| **attribution_basis** | User framed the failure mode (unreliable leader) and the deterministic replacement; assistant filled implementation detail. |
| **why** | Strong process design, but transcript shows design + implementation request more than a self-contained publishable case-study window. |

### 3. Fail-Loud AGUI Contract — `608687b7`
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Window** | turns 166–169 |
| **Label / score** | `strong_highlight` → borderline `outstanding_portfolio` · **8/10** |
| **portfolio_ready** | no (mentor_review on publishability) |
| **key_user_move** | After hollow `CUSTOM` SSE: “I don't want logs, logs are just a different way to hide. I want compile time failures or worst the operation to fail.” |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Typical fix is logging, defensive fallbacks, or UI-only patches; user explicitly rejected that pattern. |
| **attribution_basis** | User set the engineering standard (fail the run, typed contract); assistant implemented `agui_handoff.py`. |
| **why** | Led to typed `ActiveMemberChangedValue` + wire enforcement tests; strong artifact impact but user move is policy/rejection more than novel architecture discovery. |

### 4. Code-Owned Design Router (`run_design`) — `a0523e06`
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Window** | turns 54–56 |
| **Label / score** | `strong_highlight` · **7/10** |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **key_user_move** | “can we give it a less ambiguous decision tree or dag… formal rather than so much text” → “do A.” |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Assistant proposed Option A (Python router); user chose it but did not originate the DAG shape. |
| **attribution_basis** | User demand for formal routing + approval; insight architecture largely assistant-led. |
| **why** | High leverage outcome (`design_router.py`, Langfuse trim), weaker user-owned originality. |

### 5. Member Contract vs Leader Overload — `8fcea5ad`
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Window** | turn 25 |
| **Label / score** | `strong_highlight` · **7/10** |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **key_user_move** | “The leader prompt shouldn't be overloaded. Each tool/team member should declare their capability and contract… like coder and designer.” |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Common Agno pattern is bloated leader instructions; user correctly pushes contract to member metadata. |
| **attribution_basis** | Clear user-owned platform convention drawn from working members. |
| **why** | Good ownership boundary; limited transcript proof of durable artifact change in this window alone. |

### 6. Cross-User Session Leak — `9357f27e`
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Window** | turn 73 |
| **Label / score** | `strong_highlight` · **7/10** |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **key_user_move** | “ok then we have a big problem, data will leak cross user. User history will cross over” |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Assistant had been explaining missing `user_id` stamping; user escalated to security invariant. |
| **attribution_basis** | User named the production risk; investigation was assistant-driven. |
| **why** | Important stakes; window is diagnosis escalation, not a full fix/artifact proof. |

### 7. Prompt Anti-Pattern (One Use Case vs Framework) — `a5481311`
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Window** | turn 36 |
| **Label / score** | `strong_highlight` · **7/10** |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **key_user_move** | Critiqued preamble+handoff prompt text as “literally giving it one user case instead of information to make good decisions in all cases.” |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Prompt authors often encode single examples; user catches generalization failure in Langfuse steward prompts. |
| **attribution_basis** | User-owned prompt-quality standard on published concierge instructions. |
| **why** | Valuable coaching move; narrow scope vs portfolio anatomy. |

### 8. Dynamic System Prompt / Chaining Root Cause — `85f482cd`
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Window** | turns 129–150 |
| **Label / score** | `strong_highlight` · **7/10** |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **key_user_move** | “kill chaining or kill dynamic system prompts and not fight this… i prefer not doing dynamic prompts”; later model-tier policy (Opus 4.7, Sonnet 4.6 as medium). |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Would patch symptoms (capture scripts, prompt tweaks); user tied failure to Agno chaining + dynamic system prompt interaction. |
| **attribution_basis** | User drove investigation commands and rejected band-aids after capture evidence. |
| **why** | Strong evidence-validation arc; many turns are execution/ops, diluting a single publishable window. |

### 9. Selective Workflow Context Injection — `608687b7`
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Window** | turn 5 |
| **Label / score** | `strong_highlight` · **6/10** |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **key_user_move** | Skeptical Concierge can pick UI cards correctly with one giant prompt; proposes selective injection of workflow details by stage to shrink context. |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Generic fix is bigger prompt or more tools; stage-scoped injection is a specific reliability tactic. |
| **attribution_basis** | User hypothesis on context bloat vs routing accuracy. |
| **why** | Good framing; outcome mostly flows into later handoff work rather than proven in this turn. |

### 10. Concierge 1:1 Mapping Alternative — `a5481311`
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Window** | turn 23 |
| **Label / score** | `strong_highlight` · **6/10** |
| **portfolio_ready** | no |
| **key_user_move** | Proposed no-routing-mode design: members return JSON with UX components; Concierge maps 1:1 without bloated UI catalog. |
| **generic_llm_counterfactual** | Assistant had already proposed explicit handoffs; this is an alternative architecture the user stress-tests. |
| **attribution_basis** | User-owned alternative; assistant ultimately argued against it. |
| **why** | Useful tradeoff exploration; superseded by handoff design—historical, not decisive. |

---

## Plausible Additional Mentions (below portfolio bar)

| ID | Note |
|---|---|
| `a67063d7` | Long turn-12 spec (same-branch coder, no slices, deterministic PR-close)—mostly implementation steering after spec change. |
| `6c1904c8` | Simplify design input from b64 to file path—pragmatic simplification, not rare judgment. |
| `97ea2489` | JWT dual-verifier + audience check—good ops/security follow-through, routine once framed. |
| Subagents (`b58dc03f`, `8fcea5ad`) | User messages are “run CI tests” / “review uncommitted code”—delegation, not judgment windows. |

---

## Scan Summary

| Metric | Count |
|---|---|
| Qualifying parent transcripts | 42 |
| Subagent files with user role | 3 |
| **Total user turns** | **225** |
| Portfolio-ready in scope | 1 confirmed (`a5481311` UI-race window) |
| Strong highlights in scope | 9 additional plausible windows |
| Existing IDs in scope | 2 (`a5481311`, `82a3118c`) |
| Existing IDs excluded by prefix filter | 3 (`07653483`, `e11ea567`, `da4ffd93`) |

**Highest new in-scope finding not yet in `Arleif's.md`:** `608687b7` fail-loud AGUI contract (turns 166–169)—worth mentor review for promotion to `outstanding_portfolio` if publishable case-study anatomy is required.

[REDACTED]
